developer_response.body: Hello, we're sorry to hear you did not enjoy your time with Tunnels. To clarify, the Single Player Adventure is its own narrative adventure with content completely different than the multiplayer. For the zone reset, we will make sure to look into the issue.

The gameplay is smooth, and the mix of exploration, puzzles, and action keeps things fresh. I was genuinely surprised by how tense some moments felt—this game really uses VR to pull you into its world.
I’ve only been playing solo, im not much of a multiplayer.
Useless monster, horrible game.

reviews.review_title: Made me scream at the top of my lungs😱
reviews.review_description:
I played this game and it made throw my headset off and scream like a girl. I’m giving it five stars specifically because I came to a fork in the cave and looked both ways before a monster showed up in the shadows. Holy s&;@!

On a serious note, this game is dark. You have a flashlight and can navigate by crawling through small crevices underground. Keep your controllers near your chin while crawling as that seems to be the most effective way to crawl. 

The story, in some way reminds me a little of Fire Watch. Since it is a VR game, the story itself is more condensed, but the mystery and suspense are most definitely present.

Imagine you just got the game like me you cant play because its says loading 0% for long periods of times
just finally got in

It's a very good game. Would buy the full single player bundle. But I don't have enough money. Great game otherwise. I would definitely recommend to anybody who's searching for a first person, single player or multiplayer adventure game.

Tunnels will likely to appeal to fans of horror games who seek something a bit different from what’s currently offered in the genre. The claustrophobic atmosphere combined with a tense sense of unease, enhanced by sounds that amplify these feelings, creates a unique experience. However, while this aspect is executed well, the game does have its share of technical flaws that hopefully can be resolved through patches. Issues such as hands clipping through the environment in the tunnels and occasionally getting stuck on environmental elements like rocks and underwater plants can be frustrating, though I managed to escape these situations by navigating my play-space. Although these issues aren’t fundamentally flaws in the gameplay design, they do disrupt immersion, particularly when trying to evade monsters – and maintaining immersion is crucial in virtual reality. What is present indicates that the game has significant potential; it simply requires some adjustments, and with additional content set to be released, I hope that patches will address these concerns as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the thorough feedback! We will continue to improve the game with patches over the next few months, and we hope to address your issues.

reviews.review_title: fun but dont notice any update
reviews.review_description: tunnels 1.0 update was said to be released on may 15th and with it a bunch of updates to single player and multiplayer, but I havent noticed any difference so far, especially in multiplayer. if it turns out they havent released the update yet then I will edit my review and give them a proper rating.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thank you for expressing your concern! We would like to ask you to make sure to double-check that you have updated the game to its newest version. If you see "Adventure Mode" and "Multiplayer" on the main menu, then you're on the right track! To be specific, the multiplayer has new levels, new cosmetics, and an expanded lobby for hanging out with other players! The single player now has a full "Adventure Mode."

reviews.review_title: Why does the monster have a 🍆
reviews.review_description:
I'll admit, it's a pretty fun game.
But there's only 3 levels. and only in the first level can you repel the monster with your flashlight. The other levels? Naw he's faster and can't be repelled with your flashlight. Also the monster has a 🍆. I don't know if it was for comedic purposes but it was kinda weird when the monster would bug out for the 3rd time in a row and start digging in you till you beat the level .

developer_response.body: Hello BeeLeeX, thank you for the review! We appreciate the feedback and will consider it when we improve and add content. As for the monster design, what you're seeing is the monster's tail! We'll make sure that is a little clearer in the future.

The game isn't advertised as a demo. I don't usually play multiplayer modes in games so this was simply a waste of time. I'm not paying almost 20 bucks for single player. Get real.
Nothing like the showcase, your hands clip through walls, and the graphics were nowhere near as good.
First time I played I was screaming this game is very good but the only problem with multiplayer are people pressing the reset level button over and over but other than that game is great

developer_response.body: Thank you for letting us know you ran into a bug! We'll work to fix it in the near future. We hope it was an enjoyable overall.

reviews.review_description: This game has amazing graphics, cool storyline, and the horror is terrifying. This is a great game for free, and I would pay for this. BUT HALF WAY THROUGH THE STORY IT LITERALLY STOPS AND SAYS YOU FINISHED THE GAME. It’s a great game, but if it wasn’t just 15 minutes long and completely stopped through the storyline and make you wonder how long it’s gonna be until the next update or you can finish the story it would have been much better. I’m begging you guys. Please tell me when your next update is! Also multiplayer won’t work with me. I don’t know if that’s a bug or just nobody’s online.
I have stick drift so i cant play because you cant turn off turning so im spinning while just on a wire over a cliff please put a option to turn off turning in a update please

I've always been looking for a true caving game and this is so promising! Very good atmosphere and graphics,  mechanics... please keep the development!! I'd include randomly generated caves, more dynamics such as hitting the walls with the pike, etc. But looks SUPERB!! Cheers

I just finished playing and was very disappointed that the game stopped abruptly. I WANT MORE HAHA! Very good for a free demo. The suspense is there for sure. I’m not a jumpy person but this was great. The only time my heart raced a bit was when I fell off the first rope lol. If this was real world, hell to the no because i have claustrophobia in tight, restricted areas. Give it a try and give it a review so we can play more!!!

developer_response.body:
Hello fonejackerjon, 
According to the data coming from Meta, Tunnels has an average rate of 71 FPS for Quest 2. The game did not fall below 60 FPS in our tests as well. Can you tell us which headset you are having a problem with?

developer_response.body:
Hello chelanmaster,
After you download Tunnels from Applab, you can select Multiplayer from the Menu Tab. Once you are in you will need to enter a name for yourself and a name for the server. Then you can press Join Server and your server will be created. Your brother and cousin can enter the same server name to enter your lobby. Have fun.
